decision_policy.notes: Day12 product-facing decision layer. Baseline threshold remains the binary regression reference; final_label uses real <= 0.12, ai >= 0.18, and uncertain between them.

uncertain_decision_v2.notes: Day16 multi-resolution decision layer. These thresholds are diagnostic aggregation defaults and do not change the core detector fusion weights.

uncertain_decision_v21.notes: Day16.1 resize-bias calibrated decision layer. These values only affect aggregation over existing detector outputs; core detector weights are unchanged.

baseline.notes: Day8-compatible baseline. Runtime fusion weights preserve the current V0.1 score behavior.

balanced_v2_candidate.notes: Day9.1 candidate: lightly reduces frequency/forensic/compression influence, keeps missing EXIF weak, and avoids treating file format alone as strong AI evidence.

review_safe_candidate.notes: Day9.1 candidate: preserves baseline score behavior but expands the low-confidence margin so more near-threshold samples become uncertain instead of forced binary calls.

reduce_false_positive.notes: Lower direct AI-score impact from missing metadata, compression/context artifacts, and blur-like weak signals to reduce real-image false positives.

improve_ai_recall.notes: Increase smooth-texture, local-consistency, frequency, and edge-anomaly signals to surface AI samples that the baseline misses.
